skill.name: fda-guideline-search
skill.description: Search FDA industry guidelines by therapeutic area or topic. Fetches guidance documents from FDA and ICH databases with filtering by document type and year.
skill.summary: Search and retrieve FDA industry guidance documents by therapeutic area with type and year filters.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 87 patterns across 6 files, but all high and critical findings are confirmed false positives after semantic review. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are misidentified markdown and JSON text in documentation. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code block delimiters in SKILL.md (not executable code). No credential access, prompt injection, or command injection vectors were found. The skill legitimately searches FDA public websites via HTTP and caches PDFs locally.

medium_findings.title: Local file write operations
medium_findings.description: Script writes downloaded PDFs to a local cache directory and search results to a user-specified output file. This is expected and documented behavior for a guideline search and download tool. No path traversal vector exists as paths are constructed from document metadata, not user input.

low_findings.title: HTTP requests to external servers
low_findings.description: Script uses urllib.request to fetch data from FDA (fda.gov) and ICH (ich.org) websites. All destination URLs point to legitimate government and international health databases and are hardcoded. No user-controlled URL injection is possible. This is expected behavior for a guideline search skill.

low_findings.title: Markdown documentation code blocks
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains shell command examples in markdown code blocks (e.g., 'python scripts/main.py --area oncology'). These are documentation examples for users, not automated script execution. No command injection vector exists as there is no dynamic command construction.

content.user_title: Search FDA drug guidelines by therapeutic area
content.value_statement: Researchers and clinicians need quick access to FDA guidance documents organized by therapeutic area. This skill searches FDA and ICH databases, filters results by document type and year, and returns structured document details with download links.
actual_capabilities: Search FDA guidance documents by therapeutic area such as oncology, cardiology, and neurology
Filter results by document type (draft, final, ICH guidelines)
Limit the number of results and filter by publication year or year range
Download guideline PDFs to a local cache directory
Display all available therapeutic areas for reference
limitations: Rate limited to 10 requests per minute to respect FDA server policies
Uses sample data structure for demonstration; real FDA API access requires additional setup for production use
ICH guidelines require separate database access and may have different update schedules
Some historical FDA documents may not be available as digital PDFs

use_cases.title: Oncology clinical trial guidance search
use_cases.description: A clinical researcher needs the latest FDA guidance documents for oncology drug development. The skill returns structured results with document titles, issue dates, and PDF links filtered by the oncology therapeutic area.

use_cases.title: Regulatory compliance document filtering
use_cases.description: A regulatory affairs specialist filters FDA guidelines by type (draft or final) and publication year to track recent regulatory changes relevant to their therapeutic area.

use_cases.title: Medical writing reference compilation
use_cases.description: A medical writer compiling references for a submission searches for ICH guidelines and downloads relevant PDFs to build a local reference library.

prompt_templates.title: Basic therapeutic area search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search FDA guidelines for the {therapeutic_area} area. Show me the results with document titles and dates.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple search for guidelines in a specific medical area

prompt_templates.title: Filtered search with document type and year
prompt_templates.prompt: Find FDA draft guidelines in {therapeutic_area} published between {start_year} and {end_year}. Limit to 10 results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Narrowing search scope by document status and timeframe

prompt_templates.title: Search and download guidelines
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for {therapeutic_area} FDA guidelines and download the PDF files to the local cache. Only get final guidance documents from {year}.
prompt_templates.scenario: Retrieving full documents for offline reference

prompt_templates.title: Multi-area comparison with keyword search
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare FDA guidelines for {area_one} and {area_two}. For each area, search for documents containing {keyword} and summarize the key differences in regulatory requirements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cross-therapeutic area analysis for research planning

output_examples.output: Found 8 FDA guidance documents for oncology. Results include: Clinical Trial Considerations for Oncology Drug Development (Final, 2025), ICH E6(R2): Good Clinical Practice - Oncology Applications (ICH Final, 2016), and 6 more documents. Use --download to retrieve PDFs.
2 draft guidelines found for cardiology from 2023: 1) Clinical Trial Considerations for Cardiology Drug Development (Draft, 2023). Use --year 2020-2024 or --type final to adjust filters.

best_practices: Specify a narrow therapeutic area like oncology or cardiology for more targeted search results
Use the type and year filters together to reduce result volume and find the most relevant documents
Use the download option only when you need full document text, and set a reasonable limit to respect rate limits
anti_patterns: Searching without specifying a therapeutic area - the skill requires a valid area to produce meaningful results
Requesting downloads for all results without filtering first, which may trigger rate limiting on FDA servers
Using unrecognized therapeutic area names without first checking the list of available areas

faq.question: What therapeutic areas does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports 16 therapeutic areas including oncology, cardiology, neurology, rare disease, infectious disease, immunology, endocrinology, gastroenterology, respiratory, dermatology, nephrology, hematology, ophthalmology, psychiatry, pediatrics, and geriatrics.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to a real FDA database?
faq.answer: The skill uses sample data in its current form. The code is structured to work with FDA web pages via urllib, but production deployment would need additional setup for real FDA API access.

faq.question: How does rate limiting work?
faq.answer: The script enforces a 6-second delay between requests to limit to 10 requests per minute. This respects FDA server policies and prevents the script from being blocked.

faq.question: Can I download full guideline PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --download flag to download PDFs to a local cache directory. The script constructs filenames from document metadata and stores them in references/cache/.

faq.question: What is the difference between FDA and ICH guidelines?
faq.answer: FDA guidelines are specific to U.S. regulatory requirements. ICH guidelines are international harmonized standards adopted by the FDA. The skill searches both when using the default type filter.

faq.question: What output format does the search return?
faq.answer: Search results are returned as structured JSON with document title, document number, issue date, type, therapeutic area, and PDF URL for each guideline found.
